sections.label: Section 651
sections.original: μακρὰ γὰρ ἡμῖν ἡ ʼπιοῦσʼ ἀπουσία .
sections.translation: Yes , for long is the absence from each other , that awaits us .

sections.label: Section 652
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδʼ ὅ τι φῄς , οὐκ οἶδα , φίλτατʼ ἐμοὶ πάτερ .
sections.translation: I do not know , dear father , I do not know of what you are speaking . [*]

sections.label: Section 653
sections.original: συνετὰ λέγουσα μᾶλλον εἰς οἶκτόν μʼ ἄγεις .
sections.translation: You are moving my pity all the more by speaking so sensibly .

sections.label: Section 654
sections.original: ἀσύνετά νυν ἐροῦμεν , εἰ σέ γʼ εὐφρανῶ .
sections.translation: My words shall turn to senselessness if that will cheer you more .

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: παπαῖ . τὸ σιγᾶν οὐ σθένω · σὲ δʼ ᾔνεσα .
sections.translation: Alas ! this silence is too much . You have my thanks .

sections.label: Section 656
sections.original: μένʼ , ὦ πάτερ , κατʼ οἶκον ἐπὶ τέκνοις σέθεν .
sections.translation: Stay with your children at home , father .

sections.label: Section 657
sections.original: θέλω γε · τὸ θέλειν δʼ οὐκ ἔχων ἀλγύνομαι .
sections.translation: My own wish ! [*] But to my sorrow I may not

sections.label: Section 658
sections.original: ὄλοιντο λόγχαι καὶ τὰ Μενέλεω κακά .
sections.translation: Ruin seize their wars and the woes of Menelaus !

sections.label: Section 659
sections.original: ἄλλους ὀλεῖ πρόσθʼ ἃ ἐμὲ διολέσαντʼ ἔχει .
sections.translation: First will that , which has been my life-long ruin , bring ruin to others . [*]

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: ὡς πολὺν ἀπῆσθα χρόνον ἐν Αὐλίδος μυχοῖς .
sections.translation: How long you were absent in the bays of Aulis !

sections.label: Section 661
sections.original: καὶ νῦν γέ μʼ ἴσχει δή τι μὴ στέλλειν στρατόν .
sections.translation: Yes , and there is still a hindrance to my sending the army forward .

sections.label: Section 662
sections.original: ποῦ τοὺς Φρύγας λέγουσιν ᾠκίσθαι , πάτερ ;
sections.translation: Where do men say the Phrygians live , father ?

sections.label: Section 663
sections.original: οὗ μήποτʼ οἰκεῖν ὤφελʼ ὁ Πριάμου Πάρις .
sections.translation: In a land where I wish Paris , the son of Priam , never had dwelt .

sections.label: Section 664
sections.original: μακρὰν ἀπαίρεις , ὦ πάτερ , λιπὼν ἐμέ .
sections.translation: It is a long voyage you are bound on , father , after you leave me .

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: εἰς ταὐτόν , ὦ θύγατερ , ἥκεις σῷ πατρί .
sections.translation: You will meet your father again , my daughter .

sections.label: Section 666
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! would it were seemly for you to take me as a fellow voyager ! [*]

sections.label: Section 667
sections.original: ἔτʼ ἔστι καὶ σοὶ πλοῦς , ἵνʼ ἀμμνήσῃ πατρός .
sections.translation: You too have [*] a voyage to make to a haven where you will remember your father .

sections.label: Section 668
sections.original: σὺν μητρὶ πλεύσασʼ ἢ μόνη πορεύσομαι ;
sections.translation: Shall I sail there with my mother or alone ?

sections.label: Section 669
sections.original: μόνη , μονωθεῖσʼ ἀπὸ πατρὸς καὶ μητέρος .
sections.translation: All alone , without father or mother .

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: οὔ πού μʼ ἐς ἄλλα δώματʼ οἰκίζεις , πάτερ ;
sections.translation: What ! have you found me a new home , father ?

sections.label: Section 671
sections.original: ἐατέʼ · οὐ χρὴ τοιάδʼ εἰδέναι κόρας .
sections.translation: Enough of this ! it is not for girls to know such things .

sections.label: Section 672
sections.original: σπεῦδʼ ἐκ Φρυγῶν μοι , θέμενος εὖ τἀκεῖ , πάτερ .
sections.translation: Please hurry home from Troy , father , as soon as you have triumphed there .

sections.label: Section 673
sections.original: θῦσαί με θυσίαν πρῶτα δεῖ τινʼ ἐνθάδε .
sections.translation: There is a sacrifice I have first to offer here .

sections.label: Section 674
sections.original: ἀλλὰ ξὺν ἱεροῖς χρὴ τό γʼ εὐσεβὲς σκοπεῖν .
sections.translation: Yes , it is your duty to heed religion with aid of holy rites . [*]

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: εἴσῃ σύ · χερνίβων γὰρ ἑστήξῃ πέλας .
sections.translation: You will witness it , for you will be standing near the libations .

sections.label: Section 676
sections.original: στήσομεν ἄρʼ ἀμφὶ βωμόν , ὦ πάτερ , χορούς ;
sections.translation: Am I to lead the dance then round the altar , father ?

sections.label: Section 677
sections.original: ζηλῶ σὲ μᾶλλον ἢ ʼμὲ τοῦ μηδὲν φρονεῖν .
sections.translation: I count you happier than myself because you know nothing . Go within — it is wrong for maidens to be seen — after you have given me your hand and a kiss ,

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: μέλλουσα δαρὸν πατρὸς ἀποικήσειν χρόνον .
sections.translation: on the eve of your lengthy sojourn far from your father ’ s side .

sections.label: Section 681
sections.original: ὦ στέρνα καὶ παρῇδες , ὦ ξανθαὶ κόμαι ,
sections.translation: Breast , cheek , and golden hair ! ah , how grievous you have found Helen and the Phrygians ’ city ! I can speak no more ; the tears come welling to my eyes , the moment I touch you .

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: ἴθʼ ἐς μέλαθρα . σὲ δὲ παραιτοῦμαι τάδε ,
sections.translation: Go into the house . Exit Iphigenia .

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: παῖδας παραδιδῷ πολλὰ μοχθήσας πατήρ .
sections.translation: commits his children to the homes of strangers .

sections.label: Section 691
sections.original: οὐχ ὧδʼ ἀσύνετός εἰμι , πείσεσθαι δέ με
sections.translation: I am not so senseless ; But I think I will go through this as well , when I lead the girl from the chamber to the sound of the marriage hymn ; so I do not chide you ; but custom will combine with time to make the smart grow less .

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: τοὔνομα μὲν οὖν παῖδʼ οἶδʼ ὅτῳ κατῄνεσας ,
sections.translation: As for him , to whom you have betrothed our daughter , I know his name , it is true , but want to learn his lineage and the land of his birth .

sections.label: Section 697
sections.original: Αἴγινα θυγάτηρ ἐγένετʼ Ἀσωποῦ πατρός .
sections.translation: There was one Aegina , the daughter of Asopus .

sections.label: Section 698
sections.original: ταύτην δὲ θνητῶν ἢ θεῶν ἔζευξε τίς ;
sections.translation: Who wedded her ? Some mortal or a god ?

sections.label: Section 699
sections.original: Ζεύς · Αἰακὸν δʼ ἔφυσεν , Οἰνώνης πρόμον .
sections.translation: Zeus , and she bore Aeacus , the prince of Oenone . [*]

sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: τὰ δʼ Αἰακοῦ παῖς τίς κατέσχε δώματα ;
sections.translation: What son of Aeacus secured his father ’ s halls ?

sections.label: Section 701
sections.original: Πηλεύς · ὁ Πηλεὺς δʼ ἔσχε Νηρέως κόρην .
sections.translation: Peleus , who wedded the daughter of Nereus .

sections.label: Section 702
sections.original: θεοῦ διδόντος , ἢ βίᾳ θεῶν λαβών ;
sections.translation: With the god ’ s consent , or when he had taken her in spite of gods ?

sections.label: Section 703
sections.original: Ζεὺς ἠγγύησε καὶ δίδωσʼ ὁ κύριος .
sections.translation: Zeus betrothed her , and her guardian gave consent .

sections.label: Section 704
sections.original: γαμεῖ δὲ ποῦ νιν ; ἦ κατʼ οἶδμα πόντιον ;
sections.translation: Where did he marry her ? in the billows of the sea ?

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: Χείρων ἵνʼ οἰκεῖ σεμνὰ Πηλίου βάθρα .
sections.translation: In Chiron ’ s home , at sacred Pelion ’ s foot .

sections.label: Section 706
sections.original: οὗ φασι Κενταύρειον ᾠκίσθαι γένος ;
sections.translation: What ! the abode ascribed to the race of Centaurs ?

sections.label: Section 707
sections.original: ἐνταῦθʼ ἔδαισαν Πηλέως γάμους θεοί .
sections.translation: It was there the gods celebrated the marriage feast of Peleus .

sections.label: Section 708
sections.original: Θέτις δʼ ἔθρεψεν ἢ πατὴρ Ἀχιλλέα ;
sections.translation: Did Thetis or his father train Achilles ?

sections.label: Section 709
sections.original: Χείρων , ἵνʼ ἤθη μὴ μάθοι κακῶν βροτῶν .
sections.translation: Chiron brought him up , to prevent his learning the ways of the wicked .

sections.label: Section 710
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! wise the teacher , still wiser the one who gave his son .

sections.label: Section 711
sections.original: τοιόσδε παιδὸς σῆς ἀνὴρ ἔσται πόσις .
sections.translation: Such is the future husband of your daughter .

sections.label: Section 712
sections.original: οὐ μεμπτός . οἰκεῖ δʼ ἄστυ ποῖον Ἑλλάδος ;
sections.translation: A blameless lord ; but what city in Hellas is his ?

sections.label: Section 713
sections.original: Ἀπιδανὸν ἀμφὶ ποταμὸν ἐν Φθίας ὅροις .
sections.translation: He dwells on the banks of the river Apidanus , in the borders of Phthia .

sections.label: Section 714
sections.original: ἐκεῖσʼ ἀπάξει σὴν ἐμήν τε παρθένον ;
sections.translation: Will you convey [*] our daughter there ?

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: κείνῳ μελήσει ταῦτα , τῷ κεκτημένῳ .
sections.translation: He who takes her to himself will see to that .

sections.label: Section 716
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εὐτυχοίτην . τίνι δʼ ἐν ἡμέρᾳ γαμεῖ ;
sections.translation: Happiness attend the pair ! Which day will he marry her ?

sections.label: Section 717
sections.original: ὅταν σελήνης εὐτυχὴς ἔλθῃ κύκλος .
sections.translation: As soon as the full moon comes to give its blessing

sections.label: Section 718
sections.original: προτέλεια δʼ ἤδη παιδὸς ἔσφαξας θεᾷ ;
sections.translation: Have you already offered the goddess a sacrifice to usher in the maiden ’ s marriage ?

sections.label: Section 719
sections.original: μέλλω · ʼπὶ ταύτῃ καὶ καθέσταμεν τύχῃ .
sections.translation: I am about to do so ; that is the very thing I was engaged in .

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: κἄπειτα δαίσεις τοὺς γάμους ἐς ὕστερον ;
sections.translation: And then will you celebrate the marriage feast afterwards ?

sections.label: Section 721
sections.original: θύσας γε θύμαθʼ ἃ ἐμὲ χρὴ θῦσαι θεοῖς .
sections.translation: Yes , when I have offered a sacrifice required by the gods of me .

sections.label: Section 722
sections.original: ἡμεῖς δὲ θοίνην ποῦ γυναιξὶ θήσομεν ;
sections.translation: But where am I to make ready the feast for the women ?

sections.label: Section 723
sections.original: ἐνθάδε παρʼ εὐπρύμνοισιν Ἀργείων πλάταις .
sections.translation: Here beside our gallant Argive ships .
